_comment: 16VC 'Investor' cut. 16VC is an early-stage VC firm + venture studio investing in AI/dev-tools/fintech/enterprise-SaaS/infra/frontier-tech. Non-negotiables: 1-2 yrs as an Analyst/Associate at a VC firm, experience at a >=$100M fund, and demonstrated sourcing + diligence + supporting investment decisions. So this variant LEADS with Atento ($100M+ foundation VC arm — sourcing 14/20 finalists + validated diligence) to hit the screen head-on, then frontier-AI fluency + production AI-sourcing builds (their AI/dev-tools/no-code/prototyping nice-to-haves and 'build companies, not just invest' ethos), then 0->1 venture building (an asset for a studio) + fund-ops + market research / memos. Does NOT lead with the 'AI-app-software-is-un-investable / hard-tech' thesis (it argues against an AI-thesis fund) — reframes that work as thesis-building from primary research (the M12 lesson). Built by /tailor-resume. Never fabricate.
name: Adam Breaux
summary: Investor-operator with three years across the venture lifecycle — sourcing and diligencing deals for a $100M+ foundation VC arm, building four B2B SaaS companies from 0 to 1, and running fund operations and LP reporting on an $8M fund. Sourced more finalists than any other fellow at Atento and went 3-for-3 on validated pass calls. Pairs financial-modeling and diligence judgment with rare, hands-on frontier-AI fluency — nicknamed 'AdamGPT' by leadership in 2022 and a builder of production multi-agent systems for deal sourcing and operations. Builds opinionated, research-backed theses and market maps, including ~100 primary founder interviews mapping a sector from the ground up.

items.title: Venture Fellow / Investment Diligence
items.org: Atento Capital  —  VC arm of the George Kaiser Family Foundation ($100M+ fund)
bullets: Sourcing: Sourced 14 of 20 finalists and 4 of 7 startups selected to pitch — the most of any of ~12 fellows; screened hundreds of companies against the fund's themes and built relationships with founders across the pipeline.
Diligence: Ran screening calls, diligenced founding teams, assessed business models and commercial viability, and presented investment theses to internal committees.
Recalled by principals to support diligence on two Core Fund I deals: bottom-up market sizing, competitive analysis, and financial-projection review delivered directly to a GP.
Judgment, validated: On all three deals I diligenced, I recommended against — on scalability, market size, and an unconvincing expansion story; all three are no longer operating.

items.title: Associate — Chief of Staff to CEO / Venture Operations Lead
bullets: 0-to-1 venture lead: Took Agentech from a blank page to a funded B2B SaaS company (~$25M valuation today) — thesis through pivot to AI-powered insurance-claims servicing; built the team, coordinated engineering/product/design/marketing, and ran sales to the first $600K ARR. Now its part-time CFO and Chief of Staff. Built four companies from 0 to 1 across the studio.

Frontier-AI builds: Built a multi-agent investor-sourcing engine with a continual-learning loop (ranked by geography, thesis, and stage; enriched via Clay) that produced a pipeline of hundreds of investors, plus a Claude-based operations platform spanning CRM, call transcripts, email, calendar, and contracts that auto-drafts morning briefs, meeting agendas, and proposals.

Diligence & financial modeling: Built three-statement, returns, and unit-economics models and ran competitive analysis across 500+ companies; computed fund TVPI for fundraising. Owned Agentech's $3M seed end to end — investor memo, financial model, projections, and data room — to a fast, fully-subscribed close, and ran the same process across multiple ventures (~$10M raised studio-wide).

Fund operations (built from scratch): For an $8M fund, defined the venture- and studio-level metrics that mattered, stood up portfolio tracking, reporting, and portfolio-review presentations, and became the direct point of contact LPs come to for updates; led the annual LP meeting end to end.

items.title: Co-Founder — Aeon (bespoke enterprise-AI products)
bullets: Co-founded a practice that builds bespoke AI products for mid-market companies; own executive discovery, problem scoping, and adoption — primary-source insight into how frontier AI is actually deployed in production and where it creates durable value.
Built an investment thesis from primary research — ~100 founder and operator interviews mapping a sector's structure, emerging trends, and where value accrues in an AI-saturated market.

items.title: Founding President — starTUp (University of Tulsa VC Club)
bullets: Founded TU's first VC club and placed members as analysts on the 46VC Hurricane Venture Fund, a multi-million-dollar student/alumni fund.

items.title: Chair, Macroeconomic Committee — TU Student Investment Fund ($8M)
bullets: Implemented an ETF strategy and decision process still in use today; made buy/sell calls on macro trends and led analysts to execute.

items.title: Master of Business Administration — Finance & Entrepreneurship
B.S. Exercise Science / Pre-Med
items.org: The University of Tulsa  —  Division I Cross Country & Track and Field
